properties.remark: Graves County Sheriff shared a report on social media of a stalled vehicle and water rescue that occurred around 5 PM on KY Highway 58 at Brush Creek near the Graves and Hickman County line.
Water over US 51 between Gin Road and KY Highway 1540.
Water over KY 307 between KY 703 and KY 1478.
Water over KY Highway 780 at Titsworth Road.
U.S. 45 south in Water Valley is closed due to water over the road.
Around 6 PM, five vehicles were stranded in high water on Hopkins Road.
Emergency Management reports flash flooding has closed KY Hwy 58 East near the Hickman and Graves county line.
Video of Flash Flooding of roads and near homes in the Guthrie area shared via Social Media.
Water rescue with vehicle stranded in high water on Clayton Drive.
Photo from public of rockslide with debris on I-69 near Dawson Springs.
Water has left Obion Creek and is approaching and surrounding several homes.
Water surrounding two homes in Pembroke and over roadways.
Clarks River and Bee Creek out of bank and approaching homes in and around Murray. Numerous roadways closed in the county.
Highway 138 flooded near Slaughters.
Widespread and significant flooding in Christian County. Numerous roadways and highways closed. Water approaching homes in some locations.
Several roads in the county with water over them, or very close. Most city streets flooding in New Madrid.
Nearly two dozen road closures across Daviess County as of 4pm due to flooding according to the Public Works Department.
Widespread flooding with several roads impassable as of the late afternoon hours.
7 roads underwater as of late afternoon, mainly near creeks.
Widespread flooding surrounding mobile home on Highway 80.
Water rescue. The vehicle was in the water between Clay and Wheatcroft on KY-109.
A water rescue occurred outside Slaughters on Slaughters Elmwood Rd.
A water rescue occurred between Dixon and Clay on KY-132. Several roads remain closed early morning on the 16th that cross Craborchard Creek.
Lots of flooded roads around McLean County. One water rescue occurred on KY-797 outside Beech Grove.
Bushwack Rd north of Golconda was closed due to flooding.

2.5 inches total rainfall so far, heavy rain still coming down, flash flooding ongoing with streets, creeks, and especially low lying areas in and around the Angel Mounds area on the southeast side of Evansville.
Every state highway in and out of Hickman County has water over it in at least one location with the exception of KY 239.
Structural damage near Cutler, IL. Roof off trailer on Lincoln Street and 2 barns destroyed.

*** 1 FATAL *** Corrects previous tornado report from 3 SW Freemont. An EF-3 tornado tore a path of destruction across western and northern parts of Carter County. This tornado may have started further southwest in Shannon County, but it is unknown at this time. Significant tree damage occurred along Green Mountain Road with hundreds of trees uprooted or snapped, along with at least a couple trees that were debarked. A steel trailer was picked up and lofted several hundred yards. Several homes were completely destroyed along the path from Green Mountain Road and northeast towards the town of Fremont and further north along Carter Highway Y. The tornado continued northeast and produced additional tree damage along Highway M and Highway D as it moved into far northeast Carter County. It then crossed into Iron County.

An EF-3 tornado tore a path of destruction across western and northern parts of Carter County. This tornado may have started further southwest in Shannon County, but it is unknown at this time. Significant tree damage occurred along Green Mountain Road with hundreds of trees uprooted or snapped, along with at least a couple trees that were debarked. A steel trailer was picked up and lofted several hundred yards. Several homes were completely destroyed along the path from Green Mountain Road and northeast towards the town of Fremont and further north along Carter Highway Y. The tornado continued northeast and produced additional tree damage along Highway M and Highway D as it moved into far northeast Carter County. It then crossed into Iron County.

Corrects previous tornado report from 3 SW Freemont. Corrects previous tornado report from 3 SW Freemont. An EF-3 tornado tore a path of destruction across western and northern parts of Carter County. This tornado may have started further southwest in Shannon County, but it is unknown at this time. Significant tree damage occurred along Green Mountain Road with hundreds of trees uprooted or snapped, along with at least a couple trees that were debarked. A steel trailer was picked up and lofted several hundred yards. Several homes were completely destroyed along the path from Green Mountain Road and northeast towards the town of Fremont and further north along Carter Highway Y. The tornado continued northeast and produced additional tree damage along Highway M and Highway D as it moved into far northeast Carter County. It then crossed into Iron County.
Home reported destroyed on D Highway near the Carter/Reynolds County line. Time estimated.
Windows blown out of a house near Fremont. Possible tornado. Time estatimed.

*** 2 FATAL *** An EF-3 tornado with peak winds of 165 mph struck Carter and Wayne County in southeast Missouri. The most severe damage occurred in Leeper, Missouri, in Wayne County where several homes were nearly completely destroyed. Two people died when their camper was tossed into the nearby Black River. Thousands of trees were damaged or snapped along the path of this tornado.

*** 4 FATAL, 3 INJ *** t approximately 10:20 PM a tornado began near Missouri Highway HH in the Clearwater Conservation Area to the northeast of Garwood, MO in Reynolds County and continued northeastward for nearly 30 miles before dissipating east of Jewett, MO in Madison County around 10:57 PM. The most significant damage occurred in Gads Hill and Des Arc, where a few residences were significantly damaged. This tornado was assigned a rating of EF-3 with a maximum wind speed of 155 mph.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).

*** 1 INJ *** An ongoing tornado in Arkansas crossed into Ripley and Butler County, Missouri, where it did EF-2 damage. Along FF Highway southeast of Poynor, one person was slightly injured when their manufactured home was pushed and overturned. Traveling northeast, the tornado became up to 500 yards wide at some points, causing widespread severe damages to hundreds of trees across Ripley County. Several homes and outbuildings were damaged by falling trees as well. As the tornado approached US Highway 160 west of Fairdealing, it turned on an easterly path as it damaged trees and outbuildings along the highway. After crossing into Butler County, the tornado turned again and traveled north-northeast. Along F Highway in western Butler County, a couple homes suffered major roof damage, including uplift of most of the roof decking. The tornado lifted as it approached County Road 480 and the Lone Hill community.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** An EF-3 tornado with estimated peak winds of 140 mph impacted parts of Wayne and Bollinger County. The tornado did profound and extreme tree damage along its path. The tornado directly impacted a large campground near the Castor River in Wayne County, killing one person and severely injuring another. The most severe structural damage occurred on DD Highway where a home lost its roof and had most of its exterior walls collapsed in. Several vehicles were also tossed 50 to 100 feet. Another nearby home also suffered heavy damage. Numerous outbuildings were also destroyed. In Bollinger County over a dozen homes suffered minor to major damage.
Near DD and BCR 810.

An EF-2 tornado with peak winds of 135 mph impacted Bollinger and Perry County in southeast Missouri and Randolph County in southwest Illinois. The most severe damage occurred on the south and east sides of Perryville, MO, where a few homes were severely damaged and a school building had the exterior walls of the top story collapsed. Hundreds of trees were damaged along the path. NWS Paducah extends thanks to NWS St. Louis for this assistance surveying this tornado.

An EF-3 tornado started in western Butler County along County Road 462. It moved east-northeast along County Road 450. Some of the worst damage was located along here with one house completely leveled resulting in 1 fatality. The tornado then crossed Hwy 67 and hit a mobile home park on the west side of Poplar Bluff. From there it produced mainly roof damage to homes and businesses as it moved through Poplar Bluff. Hundreds of trees were snapped or uprooted along the path. It crossed Highway 60 and continued northeast to a little northwest of the town of Rombauer. Significant damage was noted along Highway T where at least one home was completely leveled. Hi-res satellite imagery helped extend the track a little further northeast, but it is unknown at this time if it extends even further northeast and possibly connects with the Fisk tornado. There were very strong inflow winds noted to the south of the tornado path with peak winds up to 100 mph which resulted in extensive tree damage.
Many reports of damage in Poplar Bluff. Houses and buildings destroyed. Power outages and gas leaks reported. Possible tornado.
Major damage reported to New Covenant Fellowship Church and a neighboring trailer park on PP Highway.
Report from mPING of Half Dollar (1.25 in.) hail.
Damage reported on the south side of Perryville. Possible tornado.
Multiple structures with major damage on the south side of Perryville.

An EF-1 tornado began southwest of Puxico in a wooded area along County Road 404. Traveling northeast, the tornado severely damaged an outbuilding on T Highway. In Puxico, a few homes suffered roof, gutter, and fascia damaged. The tornado ended just northeast of Puxico.
Reported near PP Highway and CR 254.
Six inch tree over Little Kinkaid Rd, east of McClure Rd .

EF-1 Tornado. Peak wind 105 mph. The tornado impacted areas in and near the community of Aid, Missouri. A church along County Road 420 suffered roof damage and damage to its front entrance way. Moving northeast, farm outbuilding were damaged or destroyed along F Highway, County Road 294, and AC Highway. The tornado lifted shortly after crossing AC Highway.

An EF-2 tornado began near Zoller Road in Jackson County just south of the Perry County line. Moving northeast, the tornado crossed into Perry County, where about 50 homes in the county suffered damage, and five were left uninhabitable, mostly in the St. John community. In the industrial park on the west side of Du Quion, one metal building machine shop was completely collapsed. Numerous wooden power poles were broken along the path, including a few high capacity electrical poles northeast of St. John on Kimmel Road. Reaching Highway 154 northeast of Du Quion, the tornado blew out the windows of the historic Paradise Church and toppled numerous gravestones in the adjacent cemetery. After snapping a few tree trunks behind the church, the tornado dissipated. Hundreds of trees along the path were snapped, uprooted, or suffered the loss of large limbs.
Power poles reported down on Highway 154. Time estimated from radar.
Multiple reports of trees and power poles down in Du Quoin.

EF-2 Tornado. Peak Wind. 115 mph. The tornado began just south of County Road 91 near the Stoddard/Scott County Line, causing roof damage to two homes and destroying several outbuildings/shops. Several power poles were snapped along Highway 91 as the tornado moved northeast and flipped several irrigation center pivots. The tornado mostly tracked through open fields and damaged several grain silos and shops before dissipating just west of Oran.
Gravestones toppled and windows blown out of the Paradise Baptist Church and cemetery. Probably tornado damage.
ASOS station KCGI Cape Girardeau MO.
Trained spotter reported a tornado just north of Benton with power poles down.
Tornado reported north of Carterville.
Multiple homes damaged, damage to Lowes, significant damage to nursing home. Possible tornado.

An EF-1 tornado began southwest of Sikeston near FF Highway. There were several roof panels from a barn scattered about along with power poles down south of the town of Browns. Moving east- northeast, the tornado damaged a few apartment buildings on Lawrence Street, then damaged the roofs of a nearby shopping center and fast food restaurant. Crossing US 61, the tornado did extensive roof and awning damage to a nursing home facility on Col. George E. Day Parkway. The tornado then damaged the roofs of several homes in a neighborhood near Plantation Boulevard before dissipating at Ingram Road.
Damage reported at a nursing home in Sikeston near George E Day parkway area. Possible tornado.

An EF-2 tornado (115 mph) began west of Vienna along Old Cypress Road. Traveling northeast, it damaged roofs of homes and uprooted trees as it crossed both Highway 146 and Old Highway 146 Loop. Along Dutchman Farms Road, several homes sustained roof, gutter, and fascia damage. Additionally, outbuildings were heavily damaged, and trees were snapped and uprooted. After crossing US Highway 45 and Interstate 24, a few manufactured homes were nearly completely destroyed and wooden power poles were broken on Canada Lane. As it continued northeast, the tornado crossed US Highway 45 a second time near Shelby Road and Dixon Lane, where additional homes suffered roof, gutter, and fascia damage. As it continued northeast, the tornado crossed Gilead Church Road and Ozark Road east of the Ozark community, where the great majority of damage was to trees. Weakening, the tornado lifted at Belvile Road just before the Johnson-Pope County line.
Drone footage of multiple destroyed buildings including mobile homes along US Highway 45 north of Vienna near Interstate 64.
Public photo of hail 1.75 inches near Ledbetter.
Severe damage to a home on Gilead Church Road. No injuries.
Report from mPING of Half Dollar (1.25 in.) hail.
Report from mPING: Ping Pong Ball (1.50 in.).
Report from mPING of Half Dollar (1.25 in.) hail.
Trees down at N Gum Springs Road and Lovelaceville Road.
Several large tree limbs down and one tree uprooted just north of Highway 60 on Maxon Road.
Tree down blocking the road on North Friendship and Guyline road.
Tree down on house in Brookport. Line damage.
Report from mPING of Quarter (1.00 in.) hail.
Roof blown off building along Owensville Road.
Roof blown off of a building along Owensville Road.

An EF-2 tornado began just east-northeast of the Tristate Speedway near Haubstadt. Some damage to outbuildings and power poles occurred, along with many trees that were uprooted as it moved northeast crossing Highway 168 and eventually Interstate 69. It produced roof damage to a church and canopy damage to a Gas Station to the southwest of Oakland City. Several trees were uprooted through town and one mobile home was severely damaged. Drone footage confirmed that the path continued into Pike County and eventually into Daviess County. The track continued for another 13.6 miles in Daviess County.
Double wide knocked off its foundation, front porch ripped off, roof ripped off.
Screen shot of security camera footage of a tornado in Oakland City.
Security camera footage showed a tornado in Oakland City. Damage reported around the city.
Tornado captured on security camera footage in Oakland City.
72 mph wind gust measurement by Mesonet site at 3:20 am.
Sporadic reports of power lines down in the Robards area.
Thirty to fourty-five seconds of quarter size hail in Clay.
Report from mPING of Golf Ball (1.75 in.) hail.
Hail just over quarter sized.
Golf ball sized hail 1 mile east of Panther Creek Park.
Report from mPING of Golf Ball (1.75 in.) hail.
Damage to vehicles and windshields from large hail.
Measured in Poverty, Kentucky.
Report from mPING of Hen Egg (2.00 in.) hail.
Report from mPING of Tennis Ball (2.50 in.) hail.
Picture shared on social media of baseball sized hail.
Report from mPING of Ping Pong Ball (1.50 in.) hail.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Golf ball size hail lasted 8 minutes.
Hail fell for about 5 minutes and nearly covered the ground.
Slightly larger than golf ball size hail.
Reported on M Hwy, 4 miles north of Van Buren.
Report from mPING: Half Dollar (1.25 in.).

An EF-1 Tornado started about two miles east-northeast of Oxly producing mainly tree damage in Ripley County. It ripped the roof off an outbuilding along Highway B. It continued east into Butler County causing damage to the roof of a mobile home and shingle damage to another house along with several trees that were uprooted or snapped. Track crossed Highway 67 and ended about three miles south-southwest of Harviell. Sporadic straight line winds of 70-80 mph occurred south of the tornado track.
Small branches broken.
Pea to quarter size hail reported 2 miles northeast of the airport.
Report from mPING: Ping Pong Ball (1.50 in.).
CGI airport gusted to 50 kt.
Report from mPING: Ping Pong Ball (1.50 in.).
Report from mPING: Ping Pong Ball (1.50 in.).

EF-1 tornado. 105 mph. 18.2 mile track. Numerous trees snapped, roof damage to homes and barns. Shrouded in 70-90 mph straight line winds to the south that eventually overtook the tornado and continued eastward into western Massac County.
Power poles snapped.
Damage to high school roof.
Report from mPING: Half Dollar (1.25 in.).
Measured at Henshaw Fire Dept.
Anywhere from pea to quarter size hail reported.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Corrects previous hail report from 2 SSW Fancy Farm.
1 inch hail reported south of Gracey.
1 inch hail reported NNW of Allegre, near the Christian and Todd County line.

Evidence of an EF1 Tornado started where Ripley County Highway K-6 dead ends and then the track continues northeast crossing Highway K between K-6 and K-7. Considerable tree damage occurred on either side of K highway. Several homes suffered damage including at least one that had rather significant roof damage. A few outbuildings were moved and/or damaged. It is unknown how much further northeast it tracked due to poor road network.

An EF-2 (125 mph) tornado began on TT Highway near the McGee community in far eastern Wayne County. Moving east-northeast, the tornado did extensive tree damage as it across P Highway northeast of McGee. After crossing into Bollinger County, the tornado intensified as it cross County Road 720, where an A-frame home was severely damaged and a church lost of much of its roof. The tornado continued eastward, doing severe tree damage along County Road 714. The tornado lifted after removing the roof of the house on County Road 618, just northeast of the community of Arab.
Report from mPING: Half Dollar (1.25 in.).

*** 1 FATAL *** EF2, 125 mph, The tornado began just northeast of Advance, running northeast along Highway 25. The tornado damaged approximately 200 structures as it moved northeast causing severe roof damage to many structures. Power poles were snapped, trees were snapped and uprooted along the path. The tornado continued along Highway 25 into the community of Delta where many of the communities inhabitants where inside a FEMA community storm shelter. The tornado severely damaged several buildings in Delta and continued northeast ending just south of Dutchtown.

An EF-1 tornado touched down near the Arkansas border in far southern Butler county and then tracked northeast passing a few miles southeast of Qulin. It then crossed over the St Francis River and moved into far northwest Dunklin County for a brief time before lifting. Over a dozen homes received some sort of shingle or siding damage. Over a dozen power poles were damaged including several that were snapped. Several grain bins were destroyed and many outbuildings were damaged or destroyed as well.
significant damage tornado confirmed.
Power poles down and roof damage to 2 structures.

The tornado began near the Dalhousie Golf Club and moved northeast into the southwest side of the city of Cape Girardeau north of Shawnee Parkway. The tornado caused tree damage and minor structural damage to apartments, businesses and homes as it moved east into downtown Cape Girardeau. The tornado then crossed the Mississippi River and snapped power poles north of East Cape Girardeau.
*** 1 INJ *** home destroyed , one injury from tornado.
Tornado reported near Cape Girardeau Fire Station 1.

An EF-1 tornado impacted rural parts of southwest Union County, Illinois. Damage was limited to uprooted or snapped trees and downed tree limbs along its path. The tornado began near Reynoldsville, moving north-northeast. It then turned on a northeasterly path south of Ware, passing just south of the Pottsville community, ending at Highway 146 just west of Jonesboro.
A brief EF-0 tornado caused damage to tree limbs and uprooted a couple trees along US Highway 51 north of Anna.

A brief EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph touched down just west of McMullin, MO, then moved eastward into the town before lifting as it crossed US Highway 61. The Heartland Application and Equipment building was completely destroyed and multiple homes suffered extensive roof damage. Several trees were also snapped or uprooted.
possible tornado.

An EF-2 tornado began just north of Buncombe, IL. Traveling northeast, the tornado caused severe and extensive damage to hundreds of pine trees along its path. One home suffered the loss of its roof, and a few outbuildings were destroyed. The tornado traveled through the Cedar Rock cabin area where it did severe tree damage as well as causing minor damage to the cabins. The tornado ending shortly after crossing Interstate 24 just south of Deer Ridge Road.
Metal building destroyed.

A brief EF-1 tornado began just east of Creal Springs, IL, along Highway 166 moving east-northeast. Damage was mainly uprooted and snapped trees and downed limbs. However, one home suffered roof damage, and a carport was also destroyed. The tornado lifted at Spring Hill Road just south of the Palzo community.
ASOS station KCGI Cape Girardeau MO.

EF-2. Evidence of a tornado was noted along Hollow Road with trees down across the road. It may have started further southwest closer to the Mississippi River but with ongoing flooding was unable to survey past this point. EF-2 damage was noted along Highway County Road 1320 where some power poles were snapped. A garage was destroyed as it was lifted off its foundation and pushed a little ways. Dozens of trees were snapped or uprooted along the path. There was a good amount of 70 to 90 mph straight line wind damage outside of the tornado track, most notably to the south of it.

A brief EF-1 tornado began just southeast of Eldorado, IL, along Highway 142 at Grayson Lane. Moving northeast, it crossed from Saline County into Gallatin County before ending just north of the Elba community along Zion Cemetery Road. Long its path, the tornado broke a wooden power pole, uprooted trees, snapped tree trunks, and removed the roof covering of a few outbuildings.

*** 4 INJ *** The tornado began near the town of Gage in eastern Ballard County, snapping power poles and doing damage to several barns and outbuildings. One barn appears to have been totally destroyed. The tornado then did significant damage to a church where a family sustained serious injuries while attempting to shelter under an awning. The tornado continued eastward into McCracken County where it did serious damage to a church just off the property of Barkley Regional Airport. Trees and power poles were damaged along with minor damage to home and business exteriors as the tornado continued east down Hinkleville Rd into northern parts of Paducah where the track ended at the Ohio River.
*** 4 INJ *** Multiple Structures damaged in the Gage Community. Multiple snapped power poles and trees. 4 injured, 1 critical condition.

This tornado began about 5.6 miles southwest of New Haven and traveled northeast where it caused some shingle and siding damage to a few homes. As it continued northeast and crossed Shawneetown New Haven road, it severely damaged a large barn. Some isolated trees were uprooted or snapped as it continued northeast. The tornado damaged a small section of solar panels on the north side of Hwy 141 just east of Shawneetown New Haven Rd. The tornado then crossed the Little Wabash River and entered White County. It then caused more tree damage and turned over an irrigation pivot just east of County Road 1450, which about where the tornado lifted.
Mesonet station EW0626 Gage.
*** 4 INJ *** multiple injuries reported, power poles and trees snapped.
reports of water flowing over road and into buildings.

EF2, 115 mph, The tornado began in extreme southwestern Massac County doing tree damage and damage to the roof of a home in extreme southern Pope County. The tornado then crossed the Ohio River and snapped utility poles before crossing the Cumberland River doing tree damage in the town of Vicksburg before crossing the Cumberland River a final time and dissipating in central Livingston County.
vehicle turned over no injuries.

An EF-2 tornado started just north of the town of St. Philip in far eastern Posey County. It tracked northeast into Vanderburgh County crossing Highway 66 and into the St Joesph area. These areas experienced some of the worst damage with several homes suffering rather significant roof damage. There was also extensive tree damage near Highway 66 with many snapped pines. The tornado crossed Highway 65 and moved just to the west of Darmstadt. Numerous homes suffered either roof or siding damage along the track. Dozens of trees were snapped or uprooted as well. The tornado turned a bit east and ended along Darmstadt road about a mile north of town. Max winds were 125 mph along the 11 mile track. Peak width was 150 yards.

A brief EF-1 tornado touched down in Blairsville, Indiana near the intersection of Haines Road and Albert Drive. It continued northeast crossing Highway 66 and ending along Effinger Road. Several homes experienced shingle, soffit, or siding damage along with some tree damage in the area. Peak winds were 100 mph along its 1.1 mile long track. Peak width was 75 yards.

EF-1, 104 mph. The tornado started just northeast of Montoux Park north of W. Mill Rd. The tornado moved northeast into the Highland area of Evansville producing minor roof damage to homes and also uprooting trees. The tornado then crossed Hwy 41 and did more minor damage to roofs and snapped many trees between the Hillsdale and Earle areas in Evansville. The tornado continued to track northeast, north of Old Petersburg Rd and caused more tree damage, damage to roofs on homes and also damage to fences and and a few sheds/outbuildings/garages. The tornado crossed I-69 around Seven Hill Rd and then crossed into Warrick County and clipped the north side of Elberfeld before crossing I-64. The tornado continued northeast and impacted a neighborhood off Wheatonville Rd where it caused roof damage to several homes along with destroying several sheds or shops. Then the tornado crossed St Rt. 68 and caused roof damage to a home north of Rosebud. The tornado then continued into Gibson County where is caused some snapped trees and damage to barns just south of Buckskin. The tornado lifted southeast of Buckskin just south of E 900 S Rd.
transformer damage reported.
AWOS station KEHR Henderson KY.

An EF-1 tornado started just to the northwest of a secondary circulation that lifted north of Darmstadt. This one began near the intersection of Saint Joseph Ave and Nuebling Rd. It tracked northeast crossing Old Princeton Road and eventually Highway 41 near the intersection with Volkman Road. It lifted in the Cambridge Golf Club area. Several trees and outbuildings were damaged along the path. Some homes suffered roof or siding damage. A grain bin was destroyed with the debris thrown roughly 400 yards into a field. Peak winds were 94 mph along the 4.1 mile long track. Max width was 75 yards.
ASOS station KEVV Evansville IN.
CR 1303 grain bins damaged, building damaged, barge tops blown into trees.
Part of roof blown off of a house and laying in the front yard near Millersburg Rd and N Eskew Rd, Boonville, IN. Possible tornado.
Report of roof damage, with water entering the structure, on Morre st. between 7th st and Forrest Ave. in Boonville, IN.

EF-1, 93mph The tornado began east of State Route 61 just north of W. North St. on the northwest side of Boonville where the tornado caused some slight roof damage and uprooted trees. The tornado then traveled northeast through the Rolling Acres area north of Boonville. The tornado crossed Eby Rd and caused some shingle damage and trees snapped or uprooted. It continued to move northeast and crossed W. Shelton Rd and Crowville Rd and continued to cause tree damage. The tornado lifted near the intersection of Hunsaker Rd and Ashby Rd. Widespread wind damage was noted in areas northeast of this location.

An EF-1 tornado began in northeast Weakley County, Tennessee, along Highway 89. Moving northeast, the tornado briefly passed through extreme northwest Henry County, Tennessee, before moving into far southeast Graves County, KY. In Tennessee, the tornado did extensive tree damage and damaged an outbuilding. In Graves County, the tornado destroyed an outbuilding and uprooted trees near the intersection of Highways 97 and 1270. Tornado continues into Calloway County, where it mainly damaged trees and the roofs of outbuildings in rural southwest Calloway County. It also overturned a couple center pivot irrigation systems. The tornado then moved into the southwest and south sides of the city of Murray, where it uprooted a few trees and damaged the gutters and shingles on a few homes. Near the intersection of Fourth Street and Sycamore, the tornado lifted the roof off of automobile repair shop and then lifted shortly thereafter.
tornado spotted on ground.

An EF-1 tornado began just west of Madisonville when it removed part of the roof of the Humane Society building. No animals were harmed. Traveling northeast, the tornado did sporadic tree damage and damaged the roof and gutters of a home as it crossed Interstate 69 and paralleled Highway 281. The tornado may have continued into McLean County near Jewel City, but the area was inaccessible due to flooding.
tornado on the ground.

Delayed Report. An EF-2 tornado began in the Pack Church community southwest of Calhoun. Moving northeast, the tornado was identified by trained weather spotters as it moved across open fields as it approached Calhoun. In Calhoun, the tornado damaged the roof of a historic brick building downtown as well as damaging the steeple of a nearby church and the roof of an adjacent home. As the tornado reached the east side of Calhoun, it snapped the trunks of several trees in the city park and damaged outbuildings at the 4H fairgrounds. East of Calhoun on Highway 136, the tornado snapped two wooden power transmission poles and severely damaged an outbuilding. The tornado then crossed Highway 250 where it damaged another outbuilding and snapped several large hardwood tree trunks. The tornado ended northeast of Calhoun near the intersection of Troutman Hills Road and Highway 798.

An EF-0 tornado began east of Murray along Highway 94, where it damaged the roof of a commercial building and a nearby church. The tornado continued eastward along Pottertown Road and through the Pottertown community, where it damaged a couple outbuildings and uprooted a few trees in wet soil. The tornado also downed several large tree limbs. The tornado ended near the shoreline of Kentucky Lake in eastern Calloway County.
Multiple homes have water coming into them in the Clinton, KY area.
Trees down on Hwy 280 near Calloway County East Elementary School.
Flooding on 45 North and several other locations throughout the county.
Late report. Barn and greenhouse damage. Trees also uprooted. Time estimated from radar.
Flash flooding in and around Mayfield, KY. Report received via social media.
Three power poles were damaged on Canton Pike between Pyle Lane and Eagle Way Bypass in Hopkinsville. The road remains closed. Time estimated from radar.
Vehicle caught in at least a foot of water on south 9th Street in Mayfield.
One water rescue on east 14th street near south Clay Street in Hopkinsville. The roof of a building collapsed due to heavy rainfall in the same area.
Significant street flooding in Hopkinsville. The Little river is out of its banks and flooding the Justice Center parking lot.
North Drive in Hopkinsville is completely underwater from the McDonalds to Glass Avenue. Nearby apartments are being evacuated. Evacuations are also taking place on Bell Street in Hopkinsville.
Road closures on Hwy 62 along with state routes 1820 and 1628 in the county due to water flowing over the roads.
Power outages reported as well.
Tornado reported on Hwy 91.
Spotter confirms a tornado heading toward Gordonville.
Water reported over Hwy 154 west of Pinckneyville.
At least 15 water rescues had occurred in Van Buren, MO. Late Report.
Delayed Report. Many downed trees, fencing, some power poles and damage to a home. Time estimated via radar.
Report from mPING: Ping Pong Ball (1.50 in.).
Water Rescue CR 410 and 413.
Water over the roadway on State Rt 21 north of US 160.
Flooding in residential areas.
Car in water on State Rt 142 and E Hwy - responders enroute.
10 roadways reported flooded, 1 water rescue (completed) and 1 rescue underway.
Several spots along Hwy 148 between Mt. Vernon and Waltonville area flooded over.

Water over the road on 160 East of Doniphan is several places with several inches crossing, B, T, W, H, K, 142 North of Naylor, 21 North about mile north of Doniphan, 21 South of Doniphan is flooded in several places. Several road in Doniphan and Naylor are impassible as well.
Water several feet deep on roadways in Jackson, MO.
6-8 inches of water running across Woodville Rd.
Moderate flash flooding. Not as bad as last night. No residences compromised yet.
A mobile home was inundated by several feet of water.
Kentucky State Transportation Cabinet shows several roads across Union County with water over them.
Kentucky State Transportation Cabinet shows several roads across Henderson County with water over them.
3 to 3.5 feet of water over Highway 3 at Pigeon Roost Road. One person had to be rescued from their home in that same area.
A dozen city streets are flooded.
Both roads in and out of Piedmont were closed due to high water over them. At least 9 or 10 roads in the city had water flowing over them at one point. Time estimated.
Water rescue along Highway 51 near intersection of County Road 288 north of Clinton.
Water rescue ongoing along Kentucky Highway 58 at Hickman - Graves County line.
4 of the 5 main routes in or out of Hickman County are closed due to high water over them.

An outbuilding was seen with collapsed walls and a broken wooden electrical pole. Several structures including the high school and some homes suffered roof damage.

*** 2 FATAL, 10 INJ *** A 16.35 mile long EF3 tornado started in eastern Stoddard County along County Highway Y, approximately 5 miles southeast of Bell City. It removed a roof of a house shortly after touching down and then continued east into Scott County. As it crossed Highway 61 it damaged approximately 10 power poles. The worst damage was found in the Sloanville Drive area where numerous homes were heavily damaged or completely destroyed, this included a brick home and several mobile homes. Two fatalities occurred along this road. Numerous more homes were either significantly damaged or completely destroyed from just west of I-55 and continuing east to Woodhaven Estates. Dozens of trees were snapped or uprooted. Many outbuildings suffered some degree of damage or were a complete loss. Max width was 200 yards. The tornado lifted just east of Highway O about 3 miles west of Diehlstadt. There were 10 injuries with this tornado as well.

An EF2 tornado touched down about 1.5 miles east of Burfordville in Cape Girardeau County. It tracked east for about 1.4 miles before lifting 3 miles east of Burfordville. The worst damage occurred along County Highway 363 where a home suffered significant roof damage. Further east along County Highway 343 there was an outbuilding with significant damage along with a mobile home that had minor damage. Several trees were uprooted along the path as well. Max width was 100 yards.
tornado reported on the ground.
debris spotted.
Structural damage to a home.

An NWS damage survey found that a brief EF-1 with peak winds of 105 mph tornado occurred near the Lamasco community in southeast Lyon County, KY. Most of the damage was in the form of uprooted trees along Highway 274, but one farm outbuilding was destroyed. Additionally a house near the destroyed outbuilding suffered roof damage. The path length of this tornado was 0.52 miles and the maximum width was 210 yards. NWS Paducah would like to thank Randy Wright of the Lyon County EMA for his assistance with drone footage for this survey.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).

*** 7 INJ *** A violent EF-4 tornado with peak winds of 190 mph impacted southern Williamson County, IL, during the early evening hours of Friday, May 16, 2025. The tornado began near the intersection of Spillway Road and Grassy Road doing mainly tree damage. As it moved eastward, the tornado intensified as it approached Interstate 57. The tornado strengthened to EF-3 levels, where it removed the roofs of several homes of the staff of the U.S. Penitentiary just south of the main prison complex. The tornado debarked and reduced to stubs numerous trees adjacent to Interstate 57. The tornado continued at EF-3 strength as it damaged homes along Highway 37 and north of Hudgens Road. The tornado reached peak intensity after crossing Market Road and into a subdivision just east of Andrew Road and north of Quarter Horse Road. Four homes on Kyler Court and Leslie Court sustained EF-3 damage. In addition, a newer two-story home with standard construction was completely swept off its foundation at the end of Kyler Court, which supported a rating of EF-4 with peak winds of 190 mph. The wooded area just east of Kyler Court saw extreme stubbing and some debarking of trees. The tornado continued eastward, destroying or severely damaging several homes at EF- 2 or EF-3 strength on Wards Mill Road. Additional homes were destroyed at EF-3 damage levels a couple miles to the east on Highway 166. After crossing Highway 166, the tornado turned to the east-southeast and began to weaken, eventually lifting near the intersection of Mauseyville Road and Tyler Bridge Road. The tornado path was 16.57 miles, and its peak width was 575 yards. There were no fatalities, but seven people sustained non-life- threatening injuries.
reported tornado on the ground.
tornado on ground.

An EF1 tornado touched down approximately 2.5 miles south of Benton in northern Scott County. It was on the ground for 1.3 miles before lifting just west of I-55 about 3.5 miles southeast of Benton. Total destruction of a detached garage/barn occurred along Highway 403. Several trees were snapped or uprooted along with a few other farm buildings being damaged. Max width was 100 yards.

An EF2 tornado started along Shaw Ovil Road where it did some minor damage to a couple homes. It continued east into Todd County crossing Hwy 189 and eventually lifting to the west of Hwy 181. The worst damage occurred along Blue Hole Road where several chicken houses were completely destroyed and a mobile home was swept away. Dozens of trees were snapped or uprooted and a few outbuildings were damaged. Max width was 200 yards and peak winds were 120 mph.

An EF-1 tornado damaged trees in a 6.78 mile long path across southeast Saline County, Illinois. No structural damage was reported. Estimated peak winds were 88 mph.

An EF-2 tornado began in far southeast Saline County and then crossed into southern Gallatin County along a 10 mile path. The most severe damage occurred near the start of the path along Gape Hollow Road, Forest Road, and Barrett Cemetery Roads, where several trees were snapped and wooden power poles were broken. The tornado then weaken to EF-0 intensity as it moved east across Highway 1 near the Gibsonia community. The tornado lifted shy of of the Ohio River at Jester Road.
Trees down.

trees snapped power lines down.

*** 4 INJ *** An EF-3 tornado with peak winds of 155 mph impacted the south side of Morganfield, KY, on the evening of May 16, 2025. The tornado began along Bunger Road in western Union County, KY, where ground scouring of newly- planted crops was seen in agricultural fields. Moving east, the tornado intensified, doing EF-3 damage to several homes on Highway 130, Robinson Road, US Highway 60, Terre Hill Road, and Wildlife Road. Some homes suffered near catastrophic damage, with most roofs and most exterior walls removed. The tornado weakened after it crossed Highway 56 moving east, lifting near Kit Harris Road. The tornado reached a peak width of around 500 yards and caused four minor injuries along its 9.94 miles track.
55 mph measured gust trees down.

An NWS damage survey found straight line wind damage associated with winds of 80 to 93 mph impacted Princeton, KY, on the evening of Friday, May 16, 2025. The most severe damage occurred in downtown Princeton, where a business lost its roof. Several large trees were uprooted, including two trees that fell onto homes causing damage. Numerous large tree limbs were downed across the city.

An NWS damage survey found straight line thunderstorm wind damage impacted parts of southeast Marshall County, KY, on the evening of Friday, May 16, 2025. Impacted communities included Hardin, Olive, and Aurora. Estimated wind speeds were 74 to 85 mph. Most damage was from scattered downed tree limbs and uprooted trees. The most severe damage occurred on Liberty School Road where several trees were uprooted.

NWS damage survey team found extensive straight line wind damage occurred in the Cumberland Shores community on the eastern side of Lake Barkley. Estimated winds were 80-90 mph. Numerous trees were uprooted, including one that feel onto a home, causing major damage. A few homes near and east of Donaldson sustained shingle damage as well.

An EF2 tornado touched down along County Highway 164 just north of I-24 and caused some shingle damage to a house and pretty significant damage to a couple of barns. It continued east uprooting or snapping dozens of trees along its path. Many homes experienced mainly roof damage along River Bend Road and Westminster Place. The tornado crossed alt 41 and snapped about a half dozen power poles. Some damage to a house and outbuildings occurred along Harry Berry Lane before the tornado lifted just east of County Highway 109. Max width was approximately 225 yards with peak winds of 118 mph. The tornado occurred within a broader area of sporadic straight line winds that swept across southern Christian County.

An EF1 tornado touched down initially at the Cape Splash Aquatic Center, where it did some damage to overhead umbrella structures and a few trees. It continued east across Kings Highway and into neighborhoods of northern Cape Girardeau. Numerous trees were damaged including some that were snapped or uprooted. Several homes experienced minor roof or siding damage. The tornado appeared to lift along or just east of Oak Hill Street. Peak winds were 95 mph and max width was 150 yards.

An EF-1 tornado with estimated peak winds of 107 mph did considerable tree damage along a west to east path along State Highway Y east of Jackson, MO. Several trees were snapped or uprooted along the path in addition to numerous downed tree limbs. A couple structures were damaged by uprooted trees. An unfinished house near the intersection of Highways Y and W had several walls knocked down by the tornado. A few other structures suffered roof and siding damage as well. Estimated maximum tornado width was 125 yards.

A brief EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 85 mph began along south Tekoppel Ave. on the westside of Evansville, IN, on Friday afternoon.
